name: Vantus Prelius
bio:
Physical Appearance: Imperial male. Wears a complete set of steel armor - full heavy protection befitting a warrior of the Fighters Guild. Wields a silver claymore in combat. Carries a pewter mug, a telltale sign of his drinking habits.
Race: Imperial
Personality Keywords: undisciplined, drinking, troublesome, ranking, unemployed, reformed
Role: Vantus Prelius is a Warder of the Leyawiin Fighters Guild - a ranking member who should be setting an example but has instead fallen into disreputable behavior. He leads a small group consisting of himself, Dubok gro-Shagk, and Rellian, who spend their time drinking and causing trouble at the Five Claws Lodge rather than taking contracts. As the ranking member of this troubled trio, he serves as the main point of contact for anyone trying to deal with them.
The Drinking Problem: Vantus and his associates have become notorious for their drunken behavior at the Five Claws Lodge in Leyawiin. Their drinking has gotten so out of hand that it requires intervention to get them back on track. The root of the problem appears to be unemployment - they need to be found legitimate work to convince them to lay off the booze and return to productive lives as guild members.
Magical Abilities: Despite his current state, Vantus possesses a leveled set of knight spells, indicating he has proper training in both martial and magical combat. His steel armor and silver claymore show he's equipped for serious work when he chooses to take it.
The Solution: In order to reform Vantus and his companions, they need to be found new jobs. Once employment is secured, they can be convinced to stop their excessive drinking and resume their duties. The contract they eventually fulfill is with Margarte, searching for Ogre's Teeth near Undertow Cavern.
After Reform: Once the situation is resolved and they have legitimate work, Vantus spends all his time near Undertow Cavern with Dubok and Rellian, searching for Ogre's Teeth to fulfill their contract with Margarte. This represents a complete turnaround from drinking at the lodge to actually performing guild work in the field.
Personality - The Fallen Leader: Vantus represents a ranking guild member who has lost his way. As a Warder, he should be leading by example and taking contracts, but instead he's become the ringleader of a group of drunken troublemakers. His pewter mug is a constant companion, symbolizing how drinking has become central to his identity. Despite his fall from professionalism, he remains the de facto leader of his small group - suggesting he retains some leadership qualities even in his degraded state. The fact that he can be reformed and put back to productive work indicates his problems stem from circumstances (unemployment, lack of purpose) rather than irredeemable character flaws. Once given proper work, he appears capable of returning to the disciplined life of a guild warrior.
author: Bethesda
